creator: Rebecca Bellan
title: (No AI) Lawyer behind AI psychosis cases warns of mass casualty risks
dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
content: AI chatbots have been linked to suicides for years. Now one lawyer says they are showing up in mass casualty cases too, and the technology is moving faster than the safeguards.
contentSnippet: AI chatbots have been linked to suicides for years. Now one lawyer says they are showing up in mass casualty cases too, and the technology is moving faster than the safeguards.

title: (No AI) AI companies want to harvest improv actors’ skills to train AI on human emotion
author: Hayden Field
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/03/268402_AI_companies_want_to_harvest_improv_actors_skills_to_train_large_AI_models_on_human_emotion_and_tone_CVirginia.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

If you've got strong creative instincts, the ability to authentically portray emotion, and are capable of staying true to a character's voice throughout a scene, there's a job listing calling for your experience.

The catch: You won't be performing in a theater, a film studio, or an underground performance space. You'd be using your talents to train an AI model for "one of the leading AI companies," according to the [open role](https://joinhandshake.com/ai/opportunities/improv-actor-ai-trainer) posted by Handshake, a company that provides training data to [OpenAI](https://www.wired.com/story/openai-contractor-upload-real-work-documents-ai-agents/) and other labs.

Handshake AI is one of a handful of companies of its kind, scrambling to provide more and more niche or specific training data to A …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/893931/ai-companies-handshake-improv-actors-training-data)

You'd be using your talents to train an AI model for "one of the leading AI companies," according to the open role posted by Handshake, a company that provides training data to OpenAI and other labs. 
Handshake AI is one of a handful of companies of its kind, scrambling to provide more and more niche or specific training data to A …

title: (No AI) Microsoft’s Copilot AI assistant is coming to current-gen Xbox consoles this year
author: Emma Roth
content:
![The white Xbox Series X](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/chorus/uploads/chorus_asset/file/25676324/twarren_xboxseriesxwhite__6_.JPG?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Xbox is getting ready to launch its Gaming Copilot AI assistant on "current-generation consoles" this year, according to [a report from _GamesRadar_](https://www.gamesradar.com/games/xbox-just-revealed-gaming-copilot-is-coming-to-current-generation-consoles-later-this-year/). Sonali Yadav, Xbox's product manager for gaming AI, revealed the news during a panel at the Game Developers Conference (GDC), adding that the company will also add the assistant to "more services that players are playing."

Microsoft has been working on its [gaming-focused Copilot assistant](https://www.theverge.com/news/628666/microsoft-xbox-copilot-for-gaming) for months now, with the company launching it in beta on the Xbox mobile app, [Windows 11](https://www.theverge.com/news/719668/microsoft-xbox-gaming-copilot-windows-game-bar-beta-test), and [Xbox Ally handhelds](https://www.theverge.com/news/682011/microsoft-windows-xbox-pc-combination-features-rog-xbox-ally-devices). Players can use their voice to call upon the Gaming Copilot if they get stuck in a game, and th …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/games/894799/microsoft-gaming-copilot-ai-xbox-consoles)

Sonali Yadav, Xbox's product manager for gaming AI, revealed the news during a panel at the Game Developers Conference (GDC), adding that the company will also add the assistant to "more services that players are playing." 
Microsoft has been working on its gaming-focused Copilot assistant for months now, with the company launching it in beta on the Xbox mobile app, Windows 11, and Xbox Ally handhelds. Players can use their voice to call upon the Gaming Copilot if they get stuck in a game, and th …
Read the full story at The Verge.

creator: Reece Rogers
title: (No AI) Google's AI Search Results Love to Refer You Back to Google
dc:creator: Reece Rogers
content: The company's generative AI search tools increasingly cite its own services, like Google Search and YouTube, over third-party publishers.
contentSnippet: The company's generative AI search tools increasingly cite its own services, like Google Search and YouTube, over third-party publishers.

creator: Zeyi Yang
title: (No AI) China’s OpenClaw Boom Is a Gold Rush for AI Companies
dc:creator: Zeyi Yang
content: Hype around the open source agent is driving people to rent cloud servers and buy AI subscriptions just to try it, creating a windfall for tech companies.
contentSnippet: Hype around the open source agent is driving people to rent cloud servers and buy AI subscriptions just to try it, creating a windfall for tech companies.
